course.title: Lesson 8: The Best and the Worst

segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Why is Joe's garden the most beautiful one in the town?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么乔的花园是镇上最漂亮的？

segments.text: Joe Sanders has the most beautiful garden in our town.

translations.zh-Hans: 乔·桑德斯拥有我们镇上最漂亮的花园。

segments.text: Nearly everybody enters for the nicest garden competition each year.

translations.zh-Hans: 每年几乎人人都参加“最佳花园”竞赛。

segments.text: Bill Frith's garden is larger than Joe's.

translations.zh-Hans: 比尔·弗里斯的花园比乔的花园大。

segments.text: Bill works harder than Joe and grows more flowers and vegetables.

translations.zh-Hans: 比尔比乔更勤奋，种的花和蔬菜也更多。

segments.text: But Joe's garden is more interesting.
He has made neat paths and has built a wooden bridge over a pool.

translations.zh-Hans: 他修筑了整洁的小径，还在池塘上架了一座木桥。

segments.text: I like gardens too, but I do not like hard work.

translations.zh-Hans: 我也喜欢花园，但我不愿意辛勤劳动。

segments.text: Every year I enter for the garden competition too, and I always win a little prize for the worst garden in the town.

translations.zh-Hans: 每年我也参加花园竞赛，而且总能获得镇上最差花园的小奖。
